observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive formulate clear-cut policies and well-defined guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/ financial assistance, which shall provide among others, the following:
• A clear definition of the qualified beneficiaries and what kind of assistance or donation is chargeable against a specific fund;
• The criteria in determining the amount of donation to be given in every case and the designated person/s that shall be responsible to determine the same; and
• Complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation/assistance. More-over, apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs/projects should be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the whole constituency.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant informed the Audit Team that the City is on the process of formulating the guidelines on the grant of financial assistance.
observations.reason: No clear-cut policies and well-defined guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/ financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City officials and employees concerned to submit the list of all ongoing projects and those government PPAs to be implemented to the COA and likewise inform the audit team of the posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified in the aforementioned circular for timely validation of the same.
observations.managementAction: Not acted upon by the Management.
observations.reason: The City Engineer still failed to submit the required reports and no information was provided to the Audit Team that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer  submit to COA the detail orders and accomplishment reports of all JO personnel for the Calendar Year 2014 for further verification and validation, and monitor and evaluate the performance of the JOs in their assigned projects, programs and offices to ensure that the salaries and wages paid were not put to waste and the JOs are not performing the functions pertaining to the plantilla position personnel;City Mayor, Department Heads, City Vice Mayor, and Sangguniang Panlungsod Councilor justify the necessity of  hiring JO personnel and the authority  to render services on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays, except for those whose designation requires to do so; and City Accountant and the Treasurer refrain from processing and paying claims for wages with incomplete documentations.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant informed that  in lieu of the required individual accomplishment reports they will submit a list of accomplishment of the JOs per department which certified by the department heads concerned.
observations.reason:
The list  of accomplishment of the JOs per department as certified by the department heads concerned is still not prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

According to the City Accountant, some of the JOs concerned were no longer connected with the Management, thus added difficulty in  collating the accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create a team to investigate the misuse of funds and cause the settlement of the disallowed transactions.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant justified that City officials are in good faith in utilizing the unexpended balance of LDRRMF, hence, an authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod was secured and during that time the City was left with no other option but to augment the unexpended balance of calamity fund to meet their obligations for calendar year         (CY) 2011 pertaining mostly to repairs and maintenance and fuel consumption of garbage/dump trucks, office supplies, wages of Job Order personnel, performance incentive bonuses and other important expenditures. During that time the City was in a desperate situation and they have decided to use the fund since their obligation to the people of Calapan is far greater than any law that obliges them.
observations.reason: The justification will be subjected for reevaluation of the Audit Team once the pertinent documents and records become available.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Officials who failed to provide appropriation be held liable for violating Sections 85, 86 and 87 of  PD No. 1445.

We further recommended that for the subsequent projects, the City Government refrain from entering into contract without the existence of appropriation duly obligated for the purpose and comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 85, 86 and 87 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer justified that the construction of City College Building Phase II was among the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) loan funded projects of the City Government of Calapan (CGC), and together with the LBP approved projects for the CGC, construction of the New City Public Market for ₱200 million, the approved budget for the contract for the Construction of the City College Building Phase II was ₱15 million based on LBP issued Notice of Loan Approval (NOLA).
observations.reason: The justification will be subjected for reevaluation of the Audit Team once the pertinent documents and records become available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned City officials be held liable for using the appropriation and allotment received in December 2011 to augment the appropriation for the construction of the City College Building that was completed in          July 2011.

The City Government of Calapan has no option but to allocate fund from other sources to augment the amount of ₱1,854,688.00 to sufficiently fund the ongoing project and abide by its obligation as stipulated in the perfected contract.
observations.reason: The justification will be subjected for reevaluation of the Audit Team once the pertinent documents and records become available.

If the income of the CRZP cannot fully fund the PS requirement and the current operating expenses, the City may integrate the services and funding of such economic enterprise into the regular Local Government Unit (LGU) operation pursuant to Section 3.4 of the Local Budget Circular No. 100 dated                  October 1, 2012.

observations.reason: Losses are still incurred as per disclosure with the CEEM despite measures and strategies adopted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

a. Concerned City Councilors satisfactorily explain the necessity of procuring additional multi cabs as service vehicles while still having service vehicles assigned to them; and

b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)                      (i) satisfactorily explain the deficiencies noted; (ii) refrain from splitting of government contracts; and           

(c) henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 for the procurement of logistics.
observations.managementAction:
The City Councilors concerned explained that the units of multicab are being used by their respective legal and research development staffs in their field work. Those service vehicles have been proven vital in delivering effective legislation as there is a need to reach the far flunk areas so that the ordinances and resolutions they created and formulated will be effective and operative not just in the urban areas but also in the rural areas which needs more attention considering that most of them belong to the marginalized sector of the society. The Toyota Innova assigned to them are used as their service vehicle in their constituency work such as attending committee hearing and meetings, public hearings and consultation while units of multicab are being use by their staffs in gathering information from different barangays which are all aimed in one goal which is effective legislation.

The said vehicles were fully utilized for legislative work and used as tools for delivering effective legislation. The Sangguniang Panlungsod ng Calapan are committed on delivering legislation that will affect not only those living in the urban areas but also those in the rural areas which needs more attention considering that most of them belong to the marginalized sector of the society. Hence, the procurement of another service vehicle to maximize time and to cover more area in the least time possible.

They already removed the names of the City Councilors from the units and affixed the sign “For Official Use Only” in compliance with existing rules and regulations.

With regard to the deficiencies noted on the documentation, it was reasoned out that these were “short” lapses on the part offices concerned. 

In the case of procurement of the units of multicab, it may look like there is splitting of contracts based on the PO. However, the procurement of multicab was made thru public bidding as the mode of procurement.
observations.reason: The Audit Team will reevaluate the justifications submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. City Treasurer require all the SDO to maintain their cashbook; and

b. City Accountant (i) remind the employees with CAs to liquidate their CAs within the prescribed period; (ii) closely monitor the liquidation of CAs;                     (iii) immediately issue demand letters to the officials and employees with outstanding CAs; (iv) refrain from granting additional CA to officers and employees with outstanding CAs; and (v) submit the necessary documents for the liquidation of CA totaling ₱1,467,052.48.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer issued  Memorandum        No. 3 series of 2018 dated March 12, 2019 to  SDO requiring him to maintain cashbooks for the cash granted to him. Accordingly, he is now maintaining his own cashbook.

The City Accountant             (i) closely monitored the liquidation of CAs by issuing notices to concerned officers and employees regarding the prescribed liquidation date, (ii) issued demand letters and refrained from granting additional CA to officers and employees with outstanding CAs. Furthermore, the salaries of officers and employees who still failed to liquidate despite the receipt of the demand letters were deducted by their outstanding CAs. Notices of deduction were issued to the Human Resource and Management Office; and (iii) informed the Audit Team that no individual boat ticket was issued but only the list of delegates were submitted together with the copy of the original Official Receipts issued by Montengro and 2Go shipping lines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to (a) analyze, confirm and ascertain the validity of the long outstanding liabilities in the books of accounts;       (b) if found with valid claims, notify the claimants and for those without valid documentation and/or which have been outstanding for more than two years or more and against which no actual claim has been filed, proper adjustment in the books of accounts be made and the balances under general fund may be reverted to unappropriated surplus; and               (c) henceforth, maintain subsidiary ledgers for each creditor to monitor the transactions.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accounting and Internal Audit (CAIAD) implemented the electronic New Government Accounting System (e-NGAS) roll-out hence, SLs are provided for each major account. 

Long outstanding payables are regularly evaluated and any items proved to be without valid claims were reverted to savings. 

Verification disclosed that the amount of ₱2,561,546.97 was no longer outstanding in the books.

The City General Services Officer (CGSO) manifested that efforts were exerted to reconcile the discrepancies. 

The City General Services Department (CGSD) (a) issued various memoranda to all Department Heads/Program Managers to               facilitate efficient general inventory and        remind on the rules and regulation on supply and property management;         (b) prepared and updated Property Cards to regularly monitor the movement of PPE; (c) Prepared and submitted Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks (RPCLRN) complying with the appropriate format; (d) Furnished the following: 
(i) Forms with corresponding instructions to all employees: Property Return Slip, Property Transfer Report and Report of Lost, Stolen, Damaged, or Destroyed Property to facilitate systematic inventory of all PPE; and (ii) List of all equipment accounted to respective employees in each department to secure the conditions of each property and subsequently, issue updated and renewed Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to facilitate systematic inventory of all PPE; and (e) Submitted the documents regarding the loss of firearms such as (i) Philippine National Police (PNP) Investigation Report, (ii) PNP Certification on the lost firearms;             (iii) Affidavit of Loss; (iv) PNP Blotter; and (v) payment of City Government for the firearms fees/loss to PNP totaling ₱20,000.00.

The City Engineering and Public Works Department (CEPWD)             (a) partially provided the CGSD with the description and cost segregation of road components for road networks since they are completing the working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components; and       (b) prepared and submitted the Local Road Map and working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components.

The City Accounting and Internal Audit Department (CAIAD) (a) implemented the electronic New Government Accounting System (e-NGAS) roll-out hence, the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC), Work, Other Animals and Breeding Stock Ledger Card (WOABSLC), Local Road Networks Ledger Cards (LRNLC) and subsidiary records for all PPEs and road can now be easily generated; and (b) reclassified various PPE accounts totaling ₱24,425,170.27 to its appropriate PPE accounts under unnumbered Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) dated                  August 31, 2018.
observations.reason:
The CGSD are still collating the documents needed for various properties lost and damaged by calamities or other causes amounting to ₱18,337,861.70 pursuant to COA Circular                  No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. 

The following reports although prepared were not properly accomplished:         (i) RPCPPE;        (ii) the PPE schedule and subsidiary records maintained for road networks were not distributed to its sub-components for proper facilitation of the computation of the depreciation at the end of each year; (iii) Local Road Network Property Cards (LRNPC); and (iv) road networks are not categorized to road lot, road pavement, drainage and slope protection structures and other miscellaneous structures.
     
The City Accountant still failed to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the         lost and damaged property and total road network system.

The concerned accountable officers still failed to file request for relief of accountability. 

The RPCLRN was prepared for CYs 2011 and 2015 to 2018 only due to partial information provided by the CEPWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to (a) maintain SL for each fund transfer/fund received from agencies to facilitate monitoring of the account; and               (b) analyze the status of the dormant account totaling ₱153,355.03 for proper disposition.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting and Internal Audit (CAIAD) implemented the electronic New Government Accounting System (e-NGAS) roll-out hence, SLs are provided for each major account. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. City Assessor comply with the provisions set forth in the Local Government Code of 1991 and          DOF-DILG JMC No. 2010-01 relative the conduct of general revision of assessment and updating of Schedule of Market Value (SMV); and

b. City Mayor make representation with the Sangguniang Panlungsod relative to the enactment of appropriate ordinance adopting the proposed schedule of fair market values.
observations.managementAction:
The City Assessor informed that the City had General Revision of Property Assessment implemented by      CY 2011 using 2008 based year of evaluation or sales data enacted under Resolution           No. 1417 Ordinance No. 51 dated June 2, 2010. Due to some technicalities, it was temporarily suspended under Special Order No. 2 on August 29, 2012, thus reverting back the tax computation based on the old Schedule of Fair Market Value (SFMC) of GRA 1997.  He further stated that from CY 2014 up to present his office has been constantly exerting its effort to update Calapan City’s SFMVs.  On the 2nd Committee Hearing held on November 26, 2018 it was unanimously agreed upon after the May 2019 elections, public hearing will be conducted for each sub-classification of properties. The City Assessor alone has no power in implementing the GRA. Initial steps have been done and they are now currently in the second year of the assessment calendar. Thus, to be able to proceed for the next step there political will must be involved.

The City Mayor Office informed that the City Mayor has been consistent in expressing the need for the proposed ordinance to be enacted. The SP has already conducted a series of committee hearings for this purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer and City Economic Enterprise Manager require the             (i) assigned permanent employee as Collector who shall immediately issue Official Receipts (ORs) for the amount received from stallholders to avoid misuse of funds and turnover the daily collections to the Liquidating Officer of the City Treasurer; and       (ii) Liquidating Officer closely monitor the collections of the Collector in the City Market.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer issued                   (i) Memorandum         No. 02, series of 2018 dated February 20, 2018 to their collectors to issue immediately OR for the amount received from stallholders to avoid complaints and misuse of funds and turn-over the daily collections to the Liquidating Officer; and                       (ii)  Memorandum          No. 03, series of 2018 dated February 20, 2018 to the Liquidating Officer to strictly monitor the collections of the Collectors in the City Market to avoid exposing the collected funds to possible loss thru theft or misappropriation. He further informed that the delayed issuance and         non-remittances of collections has been corrected and remitted.

The City Economic and Enterprise Manager informed that the personnel-in-charge of the collection of past due accounts will now be required to be bonded. With regards to the collection system, regardless of the request if the vendors to collect their payments in the afternoon in order for them to have enough time to sell their goods, collections will now be conducted early in the morning to make sure that the collector will be able to submit the detailed collection list and remit to the collecting officer at the City Treasury Department the total amount collected for the day and make the immediate issuance of Official Receipt.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) City Engineer                (i) monitor the actions taken by L.N. Salvador Construction, B5 Builders and Supplies and V.D. Alcantara Construction since the projects are still at warranty against structural defects for five years; and (ii) strictly adhere with the provision stated in the Items B.5 and B.7 of DPWH DO No. 22 dated               February 18, 2015 in the computation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC); and  as an alternative, (b) the City Accountant deduct from the amount due to those contractors the cost deficiency totaling  ₱1,542,826.17 or to demand payment for such amounts, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction:
The Project Engineer informed the Audit Team that B5 Builders and Supplies rectified/corrected the honeycomb under the stairs at the right side of the City College Building.

The City Engineer informed that the contractor of Rehabilitation of Ubas Street, Lalud and Upgrading of Road and Slope Protection at Barangay Tawagan will comply in correcting the defects on the surface of the concrete pavement.
observations.reason: The rectification/ correction  of honeycomb under the stairs at the right side of the City College Building  by  B5 Builders and Supplies  is subject to review and evaluation of the COA-TAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) City Budget Officer and the City Accountant review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund in accordance with Section 322 of RA No. 7160; and          (b) City Engineer (i) periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the City as basis of the City Budget Officer and City Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations; and (ii) submit a list of all completed projects to support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The Secretary of  Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP)  certified the correctness of Appropriation Ordinance No. 58 dated June 5, 2018, an appropriation ordinance approving the Supplemental Annual Investment Plan   utilizing the unexpended balance of as of December 2017 from the 20% Local Development Fund amounting to ₱4,268,252.68. The unexpended balances of current and continuing appropriations for completed projects out of 20% DF totaling ₱1,520,079.25 were included in such ordinance.

The City Engineering and Public Works Department thru the Program Manager for Infrastructure Projects regularly updates CAIAD the status of projects undertaken.

The City Accountant assured the Audit Team that he will reconcile the amounts of appropriations, allotments and obligations with the records maintained by the City budget Officer and will submit the documentary requirements to the transactions under SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. City General Services Officer (CGSO) prepare complete list of insurable property with the value of insurable properties, if determinable, to facilitate the processing of insurance coverage of property with the GSIS; and 

b. City Budget Officer include in the ensuing budget the costs of the insurance premiums of all the insurable properties of the City.
observations.managementAction:
The CGSO submitted the 
complete list of insurable properties of the City Government and insured the the same to the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Officer (CENRO) and City Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Officer (CDRRMO) (a) explain the noted observations with probable solutions to address the present conditions in the sanitary landfill;   (b) see to it that the operating criteria cited in RA                 No. 9003 for maintaining landfill are complied to mitigate the harmful effects in the environment of solid wastes generated by the locality and further damage to the health of its constituents; and    (c) ensure that  the constituents in the remaining 16 barangays burying and dumping their waste elsewhere are informed of proper waste disposal. 

We further recommended that the designated CENRO ensure that all policies, programs and activities on solid waste management of the City are fully enforced.
observations.managementAction:
Interview with the CENRO and ocular inspection of the SLF disclosed that:
(i) soil covering were now done on a daily basis;              (ii)  signage was already posted on the site;                (iii) records of garbage collectors and trucks used, and time arrived on the SLF were now available; (iv) the construction of the perimeter fence is on-going and once the construction were completed, no animals and unauthorized persons will be allowed to enter the site; (v) the drainage system were already rerouted; (vi) the concreting of the road leading to the SLF were also on going except for the NIA road;        (vii)
they are collecting residual wastes from all the barangays in the City; and (viii) they are conducting intensified campaign for proper waste segregation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. Concerned          end-users of government vehicles who failed to submit the necessary documents for renewal of registration and the City General Services Officer (CGSO) refund the amount of ₱62,843.00 for penalty incurred due to late registration; 

b. City Budget Officer include in the ensuing budget the appropriation for the premiums of insurance of uninsured motor vehicles with GSIS; and

c. CGS (i) facilitate the processing of insurance with GSIS so that the motor vehicles of the City can be adequately and reliably protected against any damage to or loss and closely monitor the registration of motor vehicles with LTO and (ii) exert effort to establish the ownership over the motor vehicles supposedly belonging to the City through coordination with LTO, Batangas Branch for eventual registration of motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction:
The City General Services department (a) explained that penalties were incurred mainly because of insufficient budget to process renewal of registrations along with unavailability of vehicles due to busy schedules. They already devised various policies and procedures to address this issues. With the improved policies, such problems have already been eliminated;               (b) issued memoranda to all Department Heads/Program Managers to  remind on the renewal of registration of motor vehicles to Land Transportation Office (LTO) on a monthly basis, and ensure proper scheduling to prevent the penalties imposed for late registration; (c) informed that the City settled the penalties in the registration of the motor vehicles by the City Government of Calapan; and             (d) submitted the 
complete list of insurable properties of the City Government and insured the same to the GSIS. 

The City Budget informed that sufficient budget is now allocated for the costs of the insurance premiums of all the insurable properties of the City.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

The BAC will continue to strictly adhere to the provisions of Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and will abide by the recommendation of the Audit Team on this matter to avoid similar deficiencies on future transactions.

The City Accountant explained that the date specified in the top portion of the Purchase Request (PR) is the implied date for which the entire PR was approved by each signatory. As to date for which the Purchase Order (PO) was accepted by the supplier, it is to his disadvantage if he chooses not to indicate any. The City monitored the compliance of delivery based on the PO date as indicated.

The proof of posting of Invitation to Bid in the PhilGEPS was submitted by the BAC.

The City Accountant explained that the date of travel was erroneous of            Mr. Lester Palomera and submitted revised Travel Order bearing the correct date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that City Economic Enterprise Manager (a) design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the low collection of income for CRZP; (b) improve the present facilities in the area to attract tourists; and             (c) henceforth, ensure that a thorough study and planning are conducted before establishment of projects to avoid adverse effect on the ongoing programs of the City. If the income of the CRZP cannot fully fund the PS requirement and the current operating expenses, the City may integrate the services and funding of such economic enterprise into the regular Local Government Unit (LGU) operation pursuant to Section 3.4 of the Local Budget Circular No. 100 dated                  October 1, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
Continuous tie-up with Department of Education to encourage their students to visit CZRP.

Upon entry of the visitor in the zoo, the staff of the CRZP is checking the official receipt issued to the visitor to determine whether the amount paid are correct.

The City coordinated to Biodiversity and Management Bureau (Parks and Wildlife).

Some of the cages of the animals were already improved. The facilities of the picnic area or the area for team building or other events, zipline and wall climbing facilities  were already improved.

City Economic Enterprise Department (CEED) is task to conduct the necessary study before an economic enterprise is establish.
observations.reason: The incurrence of losses was attributed to cost of foods of the animals particularly the tiger.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

a. Concerned City Councilors satisfactorily explain the necessity of procuring additional multi cabs as service vehicles while still having service vehicles assigned to them; and

b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)                      (i) satisfactorily explain the deficiencies noted; (ii) refrain from splitting of government contracts; and           

(c) henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184 for the procurement of logistics.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned City Councilors explained that the multicabs are being used by their respective legal and research development staffs in their field work. Those service vehicle has been proven vital in delivering effective legislation as there is a need to reach the far flunk areas so that the ordinances and resolutions they created and formulated will be effective and operative not just in the urban areas but also in the rural areas. The Toyota Innova assigned to them are used as their service vehicle in their constituency work such as attending committee hearing and meetings, public hearings and consultation while multicabs are being use by their staffs in gathering information from different barangays which are all aimed in one goal which is effective legislation.

The multicabs were fully utilized for legislative work and used as tools for delivering effective legislation. The Sangguniang Panlungsod ng Calapan are committed on delivering legislation that will affect not only those living in the urban areas but also those in the rural areas which needs more attention considering that most of them belong to the marginalized sector of the society. Hence, the procurement of another service vehicle to maximize time and to cover more area in the least time possible.

They already removed the names of the City Councilors and replaced with the words “For Official Use Only” in compliance with existing rules and regulations

The BAC adheres in the strictest manner to the provisions of Revised IRR of RA       No. 9184 in all procurement undertakings. In the case of procurement of multicabs, it may look like there is splitting of contracts based on the PO. However, the procurement of multicab was made thru public bidding as the mode of procurement. 

The deficiencies on the documentation were “short” lapses on the part of responsible staff of the concerned offices and/or of the suppliers. The BAC will continue to strictly adhere to the provisions of Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and will abide by the recommendations of the Audit Team to avoid similar deficiencies on future transactions.
observations.reason:
The Audit Team believed that splitting was resorted since four PRs and three POs were prepared for the procurement of vehicles requested within the months of November and December of 2016 even if the mode of procurement was thru public bidding.

Review of gasoline used and trip tickets revealed that these vehicles were often used by the constituents in the barangays for burial services and other activities not related to legislative activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management follow up the required authority to purchase from the Secretary of the DILG in the acquisition of the three  motor vehicles and to subsequently submit a copy of the said authority to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: The City Mayor issued Administrative Order No. 24 series of 2018 on November 19, 2018, an administrative order prescribing guidelines on hiring of contract of service and job order employees in the City Government of Calapan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Budget Officer adopt measures to properly monitor the balance of appropriations for this will serve as basis in certifying as to the availability of appropriations and allotments against which expenditures and obligations may be properly charged.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive formulate clear-cut policies and well-defined guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/ financial assistance, which shall provide among others, the following:
• A clear definition of the qualified beneficiaries and what kind of assistance or donation is chargeable against a specific fund;
• The criteria in determining the amount of donation to be given in every case and the designated person/s that shall be responsible to determine the same; and
• Complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation/assistance. More-over, apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs/projects should be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the whole constituency.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant informed that the City is still in the process of formulating additional guidelines on the grant of financial assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City officials submit the legal basis in providing hazard pay to the 26 personnel of the City Public Safety Department.

Starting 2017, the City have prepared APP for all items for procurement as included in the Annual Budget. All requests for procurement are consolidated for proper procurement planning and implementation.

Copy of APP for CY 2017 and subsequent years will be submitted to COA. However,    the APP and PPMP are still not submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create a team to investigate the misuse of funds and cause the settlement of the disallowed transactions.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant justified that City officials are in good faith in utilizing the unexpended balance of LDRRMF, hence, an authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod was secured and during that time the City was left with no other option but to augment the unexpended balance of calamity fund to meet their obligations for        CY 2011 pertaining mostly to repairs and maintenance and fuel consumption of garbage/dump trucks, office supplies, wages of Job Order personnel, performance incentive bonuses and other important expenditures. During that time the City was in a desperate situation and they have decided to use the fund since their obligation to the people of Calapan is far greater than any law that obliges them.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Officials who failed to provide appropriation be held liable for violating Sections 85, 86 and 87 of PD No. 1445.

We further recommended that for the subsequent projects, the City Government refrain from entering into contract without the existence of appropriation duly obligated for the purpose and comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 85, 86 and 87 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer justified that the construction of City College Building Phase II was among the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) loan funded projects of the City Government of Calapan (CGC), and together with the LBP approved projects for the CGC, construction of the New City Public Market for ₱200M, the approved budget for the contract for the Construction of the City College Building Phase II was ₱15M based on LBP issued Notice of Loan Approval (NOLA).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned City officials be held liable for using the appropriation and allotment received in December 2011 to augment the appropriation for the construction of the City College Building that was completed in July 2011.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer justified that the project was already bided, awarded, the contract was perfected, notice to proceed was issued and the construction started in full swing when the official of Land Bank of the Philippines informed them of changes in their approved budget for the project. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Officer (CENRO) and CDRRMO (a) explain the noted observations with probable solutions to address the present conditions in the sanitary landfill; (b) see to it that the operating criteria cited in RA No. 9003 for maintaining landfill are complied to mitigate the harmful effects in the environment of solid wastes generated by the locality and further damage to the health of its constituents; and (c) ensure that  the constituents in the remaining 16 barangays burying and dumping their waste elsewhere are informed of proper waste disposal. We further recommended that the Designated CENRO ensure that all policies, programs and activities on solid waste management of the City are fully enforced.
observations.managementAction:
The CENRO explained that: (a) soil covering was not done on a daily basis due to limited fund for backfiling but these were done now on the frequent manner possible. The spraying of deodorizer and sanitizers were done four to five times a week to lessen the odor of the garbage.
Other measures like intensive Environmental Information and  Education Campaign at the barangay level were done;             (b)  SLF signage is on the process of fabrication;   (c) Solid wastes volume recording was done after the recommendation of the Audit Team; (d) access perimeter fence establishment is appropriated on 2017 Annual Budget; (e) he informed the goat owners have to contain their domesticated animals because they will be sanctioned in violation of City Ordinance           No. 02, series of 1999 or the Anti-Astray Animal Ordinance;              (f) The presence of the residents outside the property line of the City Government of Calapan and is under the political jurisdiction of the Sangguniang Barangay. The perimeter gate that will be installed will serve as barrier from the coming-in-and-out of nearby residents to the SLF. The resting area/structures of the staffs were abolished;              (g) drainage system form storm water was re-routed through the assistance of the City Engineering Publics Works Department to ensure that water will no longer be in contact with wastes; and             (h) the existing road diameter will be addressed with coordination with the Sangguniang Barangay for widening and will also coordinate to National Irrigation Administration for concreting the rough road.

The CENR Department conducted regular orientation among Barangay Solid Waste Management Councils (BSWMC) of different barangays regarding the proper ecological solid waste management practices which includes proper segregation, waste reduction, minimization and avoidance. In accordance thereto, the respective BSWMCs were organized to equip them with knowledge in the implementation, preparation of programs and information materials including enactment of ordinances pertaining to solid waste management.
The CENRO informed that mandatory segregation will be implemented.
observations.reason:
The existing monitoring wells were damaged by Typhoon Nina. The new monitoring wells will be constructed on the additional SLF cell.

Funds are still insufficient to finance the activities of SWM such as regular soil covering and application of deodorizer and insecticides as required by law.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that henceforth, the:

b. City Accountant refrain from allowing payment of expenditures the purposes of which are considered private or religious in nature.
observations.managementAction:
The City Education Officer justified that it has been the stern disposition of the City Government to follow the approved Project Proposals. In the approved Project Proposals with the following project names and descriptions: Package fee for Miss Calapan; Opening Salvo; 18th Founding Anniversary; Calero Open Kayak Race 2016; Flores De Mayo and the Camp ID Landyard), all of their sources of funds as per approved Annual Investment Plans (AIPs) are indexed as Historical and Cultural Development Fund, particularly those implemented by the City Education Department.

The use of religiously related words in the preparation of the Project Proposals was a mere part of the vocabulary of the preparer of the project proposal. These are not meant to emphasize the partisan and exclusive practice of religion of the City Government of Calapan, but all are intended for the holistic cultural and historical development of its people regardless of religious affiliation and beliefs. Moreover, all of these are cultural and traditional celebrations the city is committed to carryout annually.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 
a. Concerned City Councilors satisfactorily explain the necessity of procuring additional multi cabs as service vehicles while still having service vehicles assigned to them; and
b. BAC (a) satisfactorily explain the deficiencies noted; (b) refrain from splitting of government contracts; and (c) henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the procurement of logistics.
observations.managementAction:
The concerned City Councilors explained that the multicabs are being used by their respective legal and research development staffs in their field work. Those service vehicle has been proven vital in delivering effective legislation as there is a need to reach the far flunk areas so that the ordinances and resolutions they created and formulated will be effective and operative not just in the urban areas but also in the rural areas which needs more attention considering that most of them belong to the marginalized sector of the society. The Toyota Innova assigned to them are used as their service vehicle in their constituency work such as attending committee hearing and meetings, public hearings and consultation while multicabs are being use by their staffs in gathering information from different barangays which are all aimed in one goal which is effective legislation.

The multicabs were fully utilized for legislative work and used as tools for delivering effective legislation. The Sangguniang Panlungsod ng Calapan are committed on delivering legislation that will affect not only those living in the urban areas but also those in the rural areas which needs more attention considering that most of them belong to the marginalized sector of the society. Hence, the procurement of another service vehicle to maximize time and to cover more area in the least time possible.

They already removed the names of the City Councilors and replaced with the words “For Official Use Only” in compliance with existing rules and regulations

The BAC adheres in the strictest manner to the provisions of Revised IRR of RA       No. 9184 in all procurement undertakings. In the case of procurement of multicabs, it may look like there is splitting of contracts based on the PO. However, the procurement of multicab was made thru public bidding as the mode of procurement. 

The deficiencies on the documentation were “short” lapses on the part of responsible staff of the concerned offices and/or of the suppliers. The BAC will continue to strictly adhere to the provisions of Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and will abide by the recommendations of the Audit Team to avoid similar deficiencies on future transactions.
observations.reason:
The Audit Team believed that splitting was resorted since four PRs and three POs were prepared for the procurement of vehicles requested within the months of November and December of 2016 even if the mode of procurement was thru public bidding.

Review of gasoline used and trip tickets revealed that these vehicles were often used by the constituents in the barangays for burial services and other activities not related to legislative activities. 

The multicabs of Hon. Pansoy and De Jesus were not affixed with the “Official Use Only” since the said vehicles are under repair.

observations.managementAction:
The City Administrator explained that these consultants are the executive assistants/advisers for projects/programs not undertaken yet by the City Government which were hired on contractual basis and charged to the appropriation for Consultancy Services.

The clear-cut policies on hiring contractual, job-order and casuals are still ongoing giving emphasis on specific duties and functions, output and deliverables, timeliness,  efficiency among others.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Budget Officer adopt measures to properly monitor the balance of appropriations for this will serve as basis in certifying as to the availability of appropriations and allotments against which expenditures and obligations may be properly charged.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the disbursements were charged to savings by virtue of an Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution granting authority to the CAIAD to use all available savings to augment any budget deficiencies may arise.

observations.recommendation:

• Complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation/ assistance. More-over, apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs/projects should be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the whole constituency.
observations.managementAction: The clear-cut policies and well defined guidelines are still in process and for approval of the City Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and Treasurer to closely monitor the liquidation of cash advances as soon as the settlement falls due by issuing a reminder or demand letters to the officials and employees concerned. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City’s SWM Board revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of R.A. No. 9003, and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) for approval.

observations.managementAction: The City received a Letter dated April 25, 2016 from National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC) on May 2018 informing them that the ten year SWM plan was approved as per NSWMC Resolution No. 320 series of 2016 subject to the satisfactory submission of additional requirements and can now implement the Ecological Waste Management System such as segregation at source, segregated collection, establishment of MRF and other recycling and composting methods within the locality and implement the specific activities contained in the plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the City encourage the barangays to establish MRFs that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that the City’s SWM Board strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9003 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction:
Continuous community organizing with information dissemination on the requirements of RA 9003 will be conducted with the objective of establishing an MRF for each barangay. Consequently, 53 MRFs were established by 46 barangays.

Aside from establishment of MRF, it is also the objective of various community organizing to attain proper waste segregation in the barangay level.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer  submit to COA the detail orders and accomplishment reports of all JO personnel for the Calendar Year 2014 for further verification and validation, and monitor and evaluate the performance of the JOs in their assigned projects, programs and offices to ensure that the salaries and wages paid were not put to waste and the JOs are not performing the functions pertaining to the plantilla position personnel; City Mayor, Department Heads, City Vice Mayor, and Sangguniang Panlungsod Councilor justify the necessity of  hiring JO personnel and the authority  to render services on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays, except for those whose designation requires to do so; and City Accountant and the Treasurer refrain from processing and paying claims for wages with incomplete documentations.

During that time the City was in a desperate situation and they have decided to use the fund since their obligation to the people of Calapan is far greater than any law that obliges them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Officials who failed to provide appropriation and allotment received in December 2011 be held liable for violating Sections 85, 86 and 87 of PD No. 1445. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Officials be held liable for using the appropriation and allotment received in December 2011 to augment the amount of ₱1,854,688.00 to sufficiently fund the ongoing project and abide by its obligation as stipulated in the perfected contract.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer justified that the project was already bided, awarded, the contract was perfected, notice to proceed was issued and the construction started in full swing when the official of Land Bank of the Philippines informed them of changes in their approved budget for the project. The City Government of Calapan has no option but to allocate fund from other sources to augment the amount of ₱1,854,688.00 to sufficiently fund the ongoing project and abide by its obligation as stipulated in the perfected contract.

observations.managementAction: The City Engineer justified that the BAC has verified and validated the eligibility documents submitted by the lone bidder and found out that the supplier/contractor has already upgraded its status from Services only as reflected in the Mayor’s Permit issued on May 13, 2011 for Services only to a higher category as reflected in the Amended Articles of Partnership issued by Securities and Exchange Commission on July 7, 2011. He also mentioned that all the documents (post qualification papers of the lone bidder) were previously attached to the submitted supporting documents for billing purposes. He had attached the certified photocopy of the said documents in his justification.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 We recommended that the:
a. CGSO:





issue ARE to the real and true end-user of the PPE;



























denote in the RPCPPE the proper location of the properties;





explain the observations noted above; and
retrieve the records and pertinent documents itemized in COA Circular No.
92-386 relative the lost properties to support the application of Relief from
Property Accountabilities.

b. City Accountant:

require the concerned accounting personnel to properly maintain PPE ledger
cards which shall contain complete description as to acquisition cost and date,
quantity and other necessary information/data;
analyze the nature of transactions and make an adjustment to properly
classify these PPE to a more appropriate account; and








disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the lost and damaged property.

c. CGSO and City Accountant:
work together in tracing the discrepancies between the inventory report and
accounting records;
prepare adjustments on affected records; and
maintain the appropriate ledger cards and reports relative to property
accounts.

d. Inventory Committee accomplish the Inventory Report in accordance with the
MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II and conduct complete physical inventory of PPE.

2.7 We recommended that the:
a. City Accountant (i) analyze the recorded Public Infrastructures; (ii) prepare the
necessary journal entry voucher to record the 25% of the public infrastructures
transferred in the registry; and (iii) prepare a subsidiary record for each
component of the road network and Local Road Network Ledger Card; and (iv)
exert extra effort to retrieve the data prior to CY 2002 to establish the correct
beginning balance of the Infrastructure Assets account;
b. GSO maintain Local Road Inventory, Road Map and Local Road Property
Ledger Card;
c. Inventory Committee conduct physical count of the road network and prepare
Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN); and
d. City Engineer provide the City Accountant and the GSO with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

3.8 We recommended that the:
a. City General Service Officer prepare complete list of insurable property with
the value of insurable properties, if determinable, to facilitate the processing of
insurance coverage of property with the GSIS; and
b. City Budget Officer include in the ensuing budget the costs of the insurance
premiums of all the insurable properties of the City.

5.9 We recommended that the:
a. CDRRMO prepare and submit to the Audit Team the LDRRMFIP in
accordance with the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-
002 to reflect the allocation for QRF in lump sum and disaster mitigation,
prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities to be
funded, as well as the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years;
b. CDRRMO (i) ensure that all the proposed programs/projects for disaster be
implemented during the year to give assurance on the readiness of the City in
case of calamity; (ii) in case there is change in the previously proposed
projects, see to it that the Disaster Council is guided by DILG, DBM and
National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council Joint Circular
No. 2013-01 on the release and utilization of the unexpended balance of
disaster fund for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as
approved by the local Sanggunian and the LCE upon recommendation the
council and in accordance with the approved disaster plan; and (iii) prepare
Supplemental AIP for the proposed projects in the Supplemental Budget.
c. City Accountant, City Budget Officer (CBO) and the CDRRMO reconcile the
balances appearing in the RAAO and the Report on Utilization of DRRMF;
d. City Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher transferring from General
Fund to Trust Fund the amount of ₱646,567.39 under Quick Response Fund
to reflect the correct balance of DRRM Trust Liability account; and
e. CBO and City Planning and Development Officer (i) strictly adhere with the
regulations cited in Local Budget Operation Manual and the provision on the
use of savings, appropriations, accounting for lawful expenditures and
realignment of funds stated in IRR of RA No. 7160; and (ii) see to it that
thorough study and planning be conducted before realignment of funds to
avoid adverse effect on the on-going programs of the City.

6.12 We recommended that the City Environment and Natural Resources Officer and
CDRRMO (a) explain the noted observations with probable solutions to address the
present conditions in the sanitary landfill; (b) see to it that the operating criteria cited in
RA No. 9003 for maintaining landfill are complied to mitigate the harmful effects in the
environment of solid wastes generated by the locality and further damage to the health
of its constituents; and (c) ensure that the constituents in the remaining 16 barangays
burying and dumping their waste elsewhere are informed of proper waste disposal. We
further recommended that the Designated CENRO ensure that all policies, programs
and activities on solid waste management of the City are fully enforced.

7.3 We recommended that the:
a. City Accountant (i) remind the employees concerned to liquidate their CAs within
the prescribed period provided; (ii) closely monitor the liquidation of CAs; and
(iii) immediately issue demand letters to the officials and employees concerned
with pending cash advances; and
b. concerned City officials and employees with CA for travel immediately liquidate
their CAs upon arrival from each travel.

amount of ₱291,539.16 representing remaining balance of DAP.

9.5 We recommended that the City Treasurer and Accountant exhaust all the
possible remedies provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016
to enforce collections of receivables outstanding as of December 31, 2016. If there are no
possible remedies, the City Mayor may file a request for write-off supported with the
documents as itemized in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to the
Commission on Audit, through the Audit Team.

a. City Economic Enterprise Manager provide the City Accountant with the Report
of Delinquent Market Stallholders with amount due and penalties to facilitate
timely recording of accrued rental income; and
b. City Accountant (a) record the receivables provided by the City Economic
Enterprise Manager; and (b) adopt the accrual basis of recording the stall fees
pursuant to the Conceptual Framework for General Purpose Financial
Reporting.

11.14 We recommended that the (a) City Mayor conduct thorough investigation on the
noted discrepancies to determine the erring collectors so that appropriate legal actions
can be filed and require them to pay the under-collection totaling ₱54,314.84 without
prejudice to other amounts that will be established in the course of further investigation;
and (b) require the City Treasurer and OIC-BPLS to adopt effective internal control
mechanisms to prevent occurrence of similar irregularity.

12.6 We recommended that the City Accountant (a) prepare the necessary
adjustments to recognize the Prepaid Insurance in accordance with COA Circular No.
2015-009; and (b) henceforth, record payment of the insurance premiums as Prepaid
Insurance and adjust the same at the end of the year to record the unexpired portion of the
prepayment.

a. Concerned end-users of government vehicles who failed to submit the
necessary documents for renewal of registration and the GSO refund the
amount of ₱62,843.00 for penalty incurred due to late registration;
b. City Budget Officer include in the ensuing budget the appropriation for the
premiums of insurance of uninsured motor vehicles with GSIS; and
c. City General Service Officer (i) facilitate the processing of insurance with
GSIS so that the motor vehicles of the City can be adequately and reliably
protected against any damage to or loss and closely monitor the registration of
motor vehicles with LTO and (ii) exert effort to establish the ownership over
the motor vehicles supposedly belonging to the City through coordination
with LTO, Batangas Branch for eventual registration of motor vehicles.

the rules set forth under COA Circular No. 2013-004. Moreover, we recommended that
the concerned officials who requested and allowed the payment of those unnecessary
expenses refund the cost of the publicity materials and t-shirts totaling ₱31,967.00 and

acquiring the 3,500 vest for distribution to tricycle drivers; and

procurement under account Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution
account.

of which are considered private or religious in nature.

and Allocation Form for submission to proper offices.

procurement; (b) prepare and submit the PPMP for supplies and materials and

21.11 We recommended that City Economic Enterprise Manager (a) design/adopt
measures and strategies to remedy the low collection of income for CRZP; (b) improve
the present facilities in the area to attract tourists; and (c) henceforth, ensure that a
thorough study and planning are conducted before establishment of projects to avoid

by the City Mayor; and

23.9 We recommended that the:
a. Concerned City Councilors satisfactorily explain the necessity of procuring
additional multi cabs as service vehicles while still having service vehicles
assigned to them; and
b. BAC (a) satisfactorily explain the deficiencies noted; (b) refrain from
splitting of government contracts; and (c) henceforth, strictly adhere to the
provisions of Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 for the procurement of
