24.11 We recommended that the City Planning and Development Officer henceforth,
(a) analyze the feasibility of all proposed developmental projects approved by the Local
Development Council; (b) prioritize the projects to be funded out of 20% DF; and (c)
adopt measures to effectively identify priority projects before inclusion of the same in
the AIP for the efficient operation and effective delivery of services to its constituents.

To facilitate the reconciliation of the substantial difference between the accounting records and the records of the City General Services Department (RPCPPE), we recommended that the Accountant designate the accounting staff tasked in the posting and updating of the PPE ledger cards to be the point person who will assist the General Services Office (GSO) personnel in the reconciliation process and adjust the books of accounts and property records, as warranted, after reconciliation. We also recommended that PPE items totaling ₱69,116,471.55 not found during the conduct of physical count under the “Shortage/Overage” column of the RPCPPE with vague and doubtful notations be verified and investigated by both the City Accounting and Internal Audit Department and the City General Services Department as to their actual condition to ensure that these assets are duly accounted for. If necessary, written explanations shall be required from the persons responsible
observations.managementAction: The City General Services Department and City Accounting and Internal Audit Department exerted efforts in reconciling the discrepancies of ₱106,320,270.23 which significantly decreased by ₱93,729,176.53 in CY 2016 to ₱12,591,093.70
observations.reason: The City General Service Officer (GSO) explained and expressed difficulty in the reconciliatio n of RPCPPE against accounting records because PPE acquisitions from CY 2003 and earlier were not available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management follow up the required authority to purchase from the Secretary of the DILG and to subsequently submit a copy of the said authority to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor sent request letter to Hon. Mel Senen S. Sarmiento, Secretary of DILG to request for the issuance of authority to purchase motor vehicle pursuant to DILG-MC No. 2013-47 dated June 3, 2013. However, the copy of Authority from Secretary of DILG is still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management conduct thorough investigation on the noted discrepancies to determine the erring accountable officer concerned so that appropriate legal actions can be filed and to avoid occurrence of similar events and require the collector concerned to pay the confirmed under collection and other irregularities established in the course of further investigations.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office conducted investigation on the collection system and subsequently installed control measures such as full control access of the system is not given any more to the collectors; the System Administrator checks and monitors the collectors during actual issuance of receipts and verifies whether the amount in the duplicate and triplicate copy of the OR is the same with the original copy of the OR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management conduct thorough investigation on the noted discrepancies to determine the erring accountable officer concerned so that appropriate legal actions can be filed and to avoid occurrence of similar events and require the collector concerned to pay the confirmed under collection and other irregularities established in the course of further investigations.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury Office conducted investigation on the collection system and subsequently installed control measures such as full control access of the system is not given any more to the collectors; the System Administrator checks and monitors the collectors during actual issuance of receipts and verifies whether the amount in the duplicate and triplicate copy of the OR is the same with the original copy of the OR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor make representations with the Sangguniang Panlungsod relative to the general revision of the real property assessments which shall be in accordance with the provisions set forth in the Local Government Code of 1991 to generate additional revenues and enable financing of priority projects and basic services.
observations.managementAction: The City Mayor sent request letter to the Sangguniang Panlungsod relative to the general revision of the real property assessments which shall be in accordance with the provisions set forth in the Local Government Code of 1991.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant follow up the submission of liquidation reports for all fund transfers made by the City Government to DepEd City Division, OMNHS and various Public Elementary and Secondary Schools for CY 2015 totaling ₱4.562 million and require refund of unexpended balance, if any. We further recommended that letter-requests for the subsequent releases/fund transfers to the DepEd City Division, OMNHS and various Public Elementary and Secondary Schools are supported with purchase requests, project designs and/or similar documents duly approved by the Chairman of the Local School Board and the City Accountant and require the submission of liquidation reports for the monitoring of the proper utilization of the Special Education Funds. Likewise, compliance with the Rules and Regulations in the Grant, Utilization and Liquidation of Funds Transferred to Implementing Agencies particularly on the prescribed accounting treatment/entries set forth in the COA Circular 94-013 dated December 31, 1994 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Department submitted Liquidation Report amounting to ₱3,854,804.04.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council and other city officials concerned to re-program those PPAs that are considered not in compliance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 and ensure that all subsequent programming/planning and utilization of the 20% Development Fund of the City complies with regulations. Moreover, we recommended that the City Treasurer refrain from disbursing funds from any of the items of appropriation declared inoperative, disallowed or reduced by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan and for Management to strictly observe the provisions under Section 327 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Installation of Pedestrian Traffic Signal Facilities at San Vicente East and Camilmil amounting to ₱452,323.94 were already re-program. The City Budget Department has already forwarded the justification letter of reconsideration on to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan relative to disallowed appropriation in CY 2015 Annual Budget Review.

observations.recommendation: We enjoined the city officials to properly plan the immediate implementation of the programs, projects and activities identified and programmed during the year out of the LDRRMF to ensure the readiness of the City in case of calamity. Further, to utilize the savings from the completed projects by funding new expenditure items related to disaster risk reduction and management which shall be guided by the afore-cited rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The City utilized the amount of ₱2,619,839.49 hence, the unexpended balance of previous years’ balances and continuing appropriation of LDRRMF in the Trust Fund reduced to ₱9,347,958.13 in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the Certified Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and for the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit all required reports/documents to the concerned agencies within the prescribed period in consonance with the afore-cited guidelines/issuances.
observations.managementAction: The City Government of Calapan prepared the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on a quarterly basis and not on a monthly basis. The quarterly preparation of the said report resulted to accumulating of balance after end of each quarter, contrary to the reporting guidelines for LDRRMF of LGUs as provided by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) revisit its organizational structure and staffing pattern to assess the necessity of hiring consultants; (b) evaluate and utilize the skills and talents of their regular employees to cut cost and to boost their morale; (c) establish definite criteria and clear-cut policy on contract of services to institutionalize merit and fitness; (d) strictly observe rules in the hiring of consultants as provided under Section 53.7 of RA 9184; and (e) require from each consultant a terminal report duly supported by relevant documents.
observations.managementAction: The City Administrator explained that these consultants are the executive assistants/advisers for projects/programs not undertaken yet by the City Government which were hired on contractual basis and charged to the appropriation for Consultancy Services. The clear-cut policies on hiring contractual, job-order and casuals are still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Budget Officer adopt measures to properly monitor the balance of appropriations for this will serve as basis in certifying as to the availability of appropriations and allotments against which expenditures and obligations may be properly charged.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the Sangguniang Panlungsod has issued a blanket authority regarding use of all available savings (PS and MOOE) to augment any budget deficiencies that may arise. The City Budget Department has explained that the blanket authority regarding use of all available savings will serve as basis in certifying as to the availability of appropriations and allotments against which expenditures and obligations may be properly charged.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from granting said benefits to the consultants concerned and require the refund of disbursed funds totaling ₱50,000.00 from them. We also recommended that the City Budget Officer strictly observe the provisions on the use of appropriated funds for specific purpose/s and thoroughly review the appropriateness of the claim before approving the Obligation Requests.
observations.managementAction: Ms. Tanyag was hired not as consultant but as a Pharmacist of the Botika ng Bayan owned and operated by the City Government of Calapan located at the Calapan City Public Market. The salaries, bonus and cash gift were appropriated to the Consultancy Service in the budget. Accordingly, they adjusted the personnel benefits in the 2017 Annual Budget. The amount of ₱30,000.00 was disallowed in audit with ND No. 2016-001(2015) Calapan City dated August 23, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and Treasurer withhold taxes on the consultancy fee based on the rate as provided in Revenue Regulation No. 30-2003.
observations.managementAction: The City complied with the recommendations by withholding income using the correct tax rates.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive formulate clear-cut policies and well-defined guidelines for the granting of all types of donations/financial assistance, which shall provide among others, the following: • A clear definition of the qualified beneficiaries and what kind of assistance or donation is chargeable against a specific fund; • The criteria in determining the amount of donation to be given in every case and the designated person/s that shall be responsible to determine the same; and • Complete list of documents to be required for each type of donation/assistance. Moreover, apportionment of funds to more beneficial programs/projects should be carefully considered in order to achieve the desired betterment of the whole constituency.
observations.managementAction: The present administration has been observing COA Circular No. 2001-001 dated Oct. 25, 2007 in granting financial assistance to NGOs/POs. They also explained that the evaluation was undertaken by the Sangguniang Panlungsod prior to the payment of claims. The clear-cut policies and well defined guidelines are still in process and for approval of the City Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from affixing any picture, image, motto, logo, color motif, initials or other symbol or graphic representation associated with the top leadership of the project proponent or implementing agency. Furthermore, the city officials concerned must refund the disbursed funds representing the cost of the polo shirts, long sleeves and vests amounting to ₱37,400.00.
observations.managementAction: The concerned official refunded the amount of ₱36,278.00 net of tax on January 12, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Secretary to the Sangguniang Panlungsod submit explanation on why the foregoing appropriation ordinances were not submitted for review to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan within the prescribed period as mandated under Sections 56(a) and 327 of RA 7160. Strict compliance to the above-mentioned provisions is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2016, the appropriation ordinances were submitted within the prescribed period. Management has explained the reasons for the delay in the submission of the subject appropriation ordinances to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City General Services Officer prepare the IIRUP for approval of proper authorities; the Committee on Appraisal of Property appraise those properties and request the disposal of the same pursuant to COA rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: The City General Services Officer has prepared the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) as of September 30, 2016. However, some motor vehicles were not included in the said report.
observations.reason: The Committee on Appraisal of Property was not yet created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant analyze the documents submitted by the Treasury Department relative to the stale checks and prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the amount of stale checks in the corresponding books of accounts, copy furnish the Treasury Department to effect the proper adjustment in the cashbooks.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office adjusted to accounts payable the stale checks amounting to ₱1,213,812.83.
observations.reason: Check No. 14837859 dated April 2007 amounting to ₱2,600.00 was still not adjusted in the books of accounts amounting to ₱2,600.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City officials submit the legal basis in providing hazard pay to the 26 personnel of the City Public Safety Department. We also recommended that the Human Resource Officer submit to the COA Auditor the copy of office orders relative to the assignment of the 26 PSD personnel in difficult locations and the like, and their monthly accomplishment report for CY 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management has ceased the grant of hazard pay to PSD personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish a procedure for identifying City Health and Sanitary Department, City Veterinarian Sanitary Department and City Social and Welfare Department personnel who are exposed to hazardous area and occupational risk including the duration of their exposure for entitlement to allowances and require all personnel who claimed excessive laundry allowance, and the personnel of CHSD detailed at the CIO to return to the City Government the excess and unauthorized payment of allowances; City Accountant and Treasurer refrain from paying hazard, subsistence, and laundry allowance to qualified personnel with incomplete documentation and withhold the appropriate taxes on the allowances of personnel who are not a minimum wage earner; and Heads of Department who claim hazard, subsistence and laundry allowances, the City Accountant, Treasurer, Budget Officer and Human Resource Management Officer implement strictly the rules and regulation on the payment of allowances.
observations.managementAction: Monthly accomplishment reports and certified list of personnel exposed to hazardous areas and occupational risk were already attached and appropriate taxes were withheld on the allowances of personnel who are not a minimum wage earner. Overpayment in the grant of laundry allowance of ₱4,326.02 was refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accountant and Treasurer to closely monitor the liquidation of cash advances as soon as the settlement falls due by issuing a reminder or demand letters to the officials and employees concerned. Moreover, we recommended that the City Administrator and Department Heads concerned require their staff to return immediately the cash advances for travel in case of cancellation or termination of the trip.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances are closely monitored by the Internal Audit Unit. Accordingly, the City Accountant issued a memorandum to all offices reminding them to their accountability and informing them of the consequences.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Development Council strictly adhere with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the programming/planning and utilization of the 20% Development Fund of the City. We likewise recommended that the City Government make representation with the LBP to provide them with the new schedule showing the proportionate amount of amortization for the two loans which will be used in the next payments and for budgeting purposes. Moreover, the City Budget Officer should appropriate debt servicing of service vehicles in the fund other than the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The CY 2016 Annual Budget under the General Fund included appropriation for loan amortization pertaining to the purchase of service vehicles and proper schedule of loans was obtained from LBP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City’s SWM Board revisit the SWM Plan and revise it by incorporating all the requirements prescribed in Section 17 of R.A. No. 9003, and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Council (NSWMC) for approval. We also recommended that the City’s SWM Board monitor and review the targets and strategies to ensure efficient management of the City’s SWM program.
observations.managementAction: The 10-Year SWMP (CY 2016-2025) of the City of Calapan was adopted by the Sangguniang Panlungsod on September 14, 2015.
observations.reason: The 10-Year SWMP was forwarded to the NSWMC for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and agreed that the City’s SWM Board monitor and evaluate the implementation of the SWM program and see to it that the requirements for the operations of the sanitary landfill are observed to ensure the continuous operation of the same. Moreover, City Officials shall review the budget set for SWM activities and revisit the ordinance for garbage collection fees.
observations.managementAction: Additional funds were provided in the 2016 Annual budget to properly maintain and improve the waste disposal facility.
observations.reason: Funds are still insufficient to finance the activities of SWM such as regular soil covering and application of deodorizer and insecticides as required by law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City encourage the barangays to establish MRFs that will receive the mixed wastes for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Moreover, we suggested that the City’s SWM Board strictly implement and monitor the collection and segregation of wastes at the barangay level as required by the provisions of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9003 and its IRR.
observations.managementAction: City Environment and Natural Resources Department is in the process of organizing Barangay Solid Waste Management Council in each barangay and was able to organize about 4 barangays already and are now successfully implementing proper segregation of solid waste at source.
observations.reason: Due to insufficient community organizers within the CENRD, limited source of funds and acceptance by the people, the segregation of waste was still not undertaken in each barangay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer submit to COA the detail orders and accomplishment reports of all JO personnel for the Calendar Year 2014 for further verification and validation, and monitor and evaluate the performance of the JOs in their assigned projects, programs and offices to ensure that the salaries and wages paid were not put to waste and the JOs are performing the functions pertaining to the plantilla position personnel; and City Mayor, Department Heads, City Vice Mayor, and Sangguniang Panlungsod Councilor justify the necessity of hiring JO personnel and the authority to render services on Saturdays, Sundays and holidays, except for those whose designation requires to do so; and City Accountant and the Treasurer refrain from processing and paying claims for wages with incomplete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Individual accomplishment reports were required to be submitted prior to payment; Some JOs were already terminated and/or have resigned. Management has submitted justification for hiring job order personnel. Certificate of Appointments are critically evaluated to identify inclusion of Saturdays, Sundays and holidays in the respective appointments. Management has submitted justification for hiring JO personnel and has taken measures to monitor and evaluate their performance.
observations.reason: Detail orders and accomplishment reports of all JO personnel for the Calendar Year 2014 were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City establish a policy guideline in setting the salary rates of the JO personnel by taking into consideration their educational qualification, skills, relevant experience and the like. Moreover, Management shall regulate the number of JOs to be assigned to department/offices by determining their needs and considering whether the regular staff of these offices are not adequate to meet the demands of the service.
observations.managementAction: An Administrative Order by the Mayor is in the process of finalization addressing the proper rates to be granted based on educational background, skills, trainings, and assignment complexity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant follow-up liquidation report from DepEd City Division and require refund of unexpended balance, if any. For subsequent releases of fund, require the School Heads to submit purchase request and/or project designs duly approved by the Chairman of the Local School Board as an attachment to the letter-request to facilitate monitoring of programs, projects and activities funded by the fund transfer from the City.
observations.managementAction: DepEd City Division was already required to submit liquidation reports for all fund transfers made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the incumbent City Mayor require the City Engineer and the City Project inspectorate Team to enforce immediate corrective measures relative to the cost deficiencies noted in the conduct of inspection of various infrastructure projects to avoid disallowance in audit. We also recommended that the City Engineer and the City Inspectorate Team immediately submit justifications on the other projects with noted deficiencies by the team of the COA Technical Audit Specialists.
observations.managementAction: Four projects with reported cost deficiencies have been corrected by the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that approval of the Local Chief Executive or his duly authorized representative and signatures of responsible officials and employees be secured on the various claims of employees. We also recommended that the City Accounting Department scrutinize the claims for salaries, wages and other benefits of the employees and determine whether there was an approval on the payrolls before allowing to draw another cash advances or crediting the personnel benefits on their Automated Teller Machine cards to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, the City Treasurer and the Disbursing Officer should see to it that payments made are properly acknowledged by the payees.
observations.managementAction: The City complied with the recommendations of COA by submitting the required documents amounting to ₱12,173,187.00 on September 22, 2016 as per Notice of Settlement of Suspensions, Disallowances and Charges No. Calapan_16-001 (13).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accounting and the City Treasury Departments to adhere strictly to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 relative to the submission of reports and accounts to the COA. We further recommended that the City Accountant immediately submit the original copy of 147 paid disbursement vouchers together with all the supporting documents to COA for verification and audit purposes, otherwise, these will be subject to issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting and Treasury Departments had partially submitted original copy of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require the City Accounting and the City Treasury Departments to adhere strictly to the provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 95-006 relative to the submission of reports and accounts to the COA Auditor. We further recommended that the City Accountant be immediately required to submit the 118 paid disbursement vouchers and all the supporting documents to the Office of the City Auditor for verification and audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department submitted 55 paid disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a Memorandum of Undertaking be executed in the granting of financial assistance to various LGUs and NGOs/POs. In case the financial assistance is for project implementation, the undertaking should stipulate the proponent’s obligation/commitment in sustaining the project and the City’s role in monitoring and inspection of these projects.
observations.managementAction: The present administration has been observing COA Circular No. 2001-001 dated Oct. 25, 2007 in granting financial assistance to NGOs/POs. In case the financial assistance is for project implementation, the undertaking should stipulate the proponent’s obligation/commitment in sustaining the project and the City’s role in monitoring and inspection of these projects. The Resolution has been one of the basic documents required from the barangays before granting them financial assistance. The clear-cut policies and well defined guidelines are still in process and for approval of the City Mayor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City School Board limit the utilization of the SEF to programs and projects prioritized in the above-cited regulations in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students could derive therefrom. We also recommended that the City Accountant implement the proper accounting of fund. Lastly, we recommended for the refund of the allowance charged to SEF.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation. The City Government has set an appropriation in its CY 2016 budget under the General Fund for the allowances paid to public school teachers charged to CY 2012 appropriations under SEF. On May 25, 2016, the General Fund transferred the amount of ₱944,000.00 to the Special Education Fund.
